section_data.summaryData: This code loads basketball player data, trains an XGBoost ranking model to predict MVP rankings based on player statistics, evaluates the model using NDCG scores, and visualizes prediction errors for recent years by generating bar plots of predicted versus actual ranks.

metadata.originalCode:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

data.selected_code:
import pandas as pd
import os


def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        url = f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/awards/awards_{year}.html"
        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)
        try:
            mvp_table = tables[0]  # MVP table is the first table
            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg


def scrape_multiple_seasons(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_stats = []
    for year in range(start_year, end_year + 1):
        df = scrape_season_stats(year)
        all_stats.append(df)
    return pd.concat(all_stats)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not os.path.exists("data"):
        os.makedirs("data")

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")
    mvp_df = scrape_mvp_votes()
    mvp_df.to_csv("data/mvp_votes.csv", index=False)

    print("Scraping player stats...")
    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)
data.summary: This code defines functions to scrape NBA MVP voting results and player per-game statistics from Basketball Reference for the years 2018 to 2025, processes and consolidates the data, and saves the results as CSV files in a local "data" directory when executed as a script.

import os

    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]

import os


def scrape_mvp_votes(start_year=2018, end_year=2025):
    all_votes = []

        tables = pd.read_html(url, header=1)

            mvp_table["Year"] = year
            all_votes.append(mvp_table)
        except:
            print(f"Could not parse year {year}")

    df = pd.concat(all_votes)
    df = df.rename(columns={"Player": "Name", "Pts Won": "MVP_Points"})
    return df[["Year", "Name", "MVP_Points"]]


def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]

    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]

    print("Scraping MVP votes...")

    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

data.updated_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Scrape advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year
    # Select some advanced stats columns, e.g., PER, TS%, WS, BPM
    adv_cols = ["Player", "Year", "PER", "TS%", "WS", "BPM"]
    df_adv = df_adv[adv_cols]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(subset=["PER", "TS%", "WS", "BPM"])

    # Merge on Player and Year
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged

data.instruction: The advanced stats should include and only include BPM, WS/48, TS%, VORP, WS and PER.
The advanced stats should include and only include BPM, WS/48, TS%, VORP, WS and PER.

        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"

        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Scrape advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year
    # Select only the specified advanced stats columns: BPM, WS/48, TS%, VORP, WS, PER
    adv_cols = ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    df_adv = df_adv[adv_cols]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"])

    # Merge on Player and Year
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")
    return df_merged

        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"

    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg
data.summary: The scrape_season_stats function retrieves per-game NBA player statistics for a specified year from Basketball Reference, filters out repeated headers, adds the year, selects only the player name, year, points, assists, rebounds, and field goal percentage columns, removes rows with missing key stats, and returns the resulting DataFrame.

section_data.summaryData: The scrape_season_stats function retrieves per-game NBA player statistics for a specified year from Basketball Reference, filters out repeated headers, adds the year, selects only the player name, year, points, assists, rebounds, and field goal percentage columns, removes rows with missing key stats, and returns the resulting DataFrame.

metadata.originalCode:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]

    return df_reg
data.updated_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Scrape advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    ]
    # Merge on Player name
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on="Player", how="inner")

    return df_merged

section_data.summaryData: The main() function loads player data, splits it into training and test sets by year, trains an XGBoost ranking model on the training data, predicts and ranks players for the test years, evaluates the model's ranking performance using NDCG, displays the results, and generates plots of prediction errors for the test years.

metadata.originalCode:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

section_data.title: Untitled

data.updated_code:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    best_ndcg = -1
    best_n_estimators = None
    best_pred = None

    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        # Predict, sort, and evaluate
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_n_estimators = n_estimators
            best_pred = y_pred

    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_estimators} with NDCG: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Assign predictions from the best model to df for further analysis/plotting
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

section_data.summaryData: The plot_ranking_predictions function visualizes the difference between predicted and actual ranks for each player in a specified year by generating and saving a bar plot of prediction errors, allowing for easy assessment of ranking model performance for that year.

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

section_data.title: Untitled

data.updated_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    # Melt the DataFrame to long format for grouped bar chart
    plot_df = year_df.melt(
        id_vars=["Name"],
        value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"],
        var_name="RankType",
        value_name="RankValue"
    )

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankValue",
        hue="RankType",
        data=plot_df,
        palette="coolwarm",
        dodge=True
    )
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs Predicted Rankings ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
